new-agent-page.subtitle: Advanced agent configuration

new-agent-page.no-skills: No optional company skills installed yet.

new-agent-dialog.ceo-recommendation: We recommend letting your CEO handle agent setup — they know the org structure and can configure reporting, permissions, and adapters.
new-agent-dialog.ask-ceo-cta: Ask the CEO to create a new agent
new-agent-dialog.advanced-prompt: Choose your adapter type for advanced setup.
new-agent-dialog.ceo-issue-description: (type in what kind of agent you want here)

help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
help.reports-to: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
help.adapter-type: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
help.cwd: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
help.prompt-template: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports template variables like agent.id, agent.name, agent.role.
help.model: Override the default model used by the adapter.
help.thinking-effort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
help.dangerously-skip-permissions: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts when supported.
help.dangerously-bypass-sandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
help.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
help.fast-mode: Enable Codex Fast mode. This burns credits/tokens much faster and is supported on GPT-5.4 and manual Codex model IDs.
help.workspace-strategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.workspace-base-ref: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.workspace-branch-template: Template for naming derived branches. Supports variables like issue.identifier, issue.title, agent.name, project.id, workspace.repoRef, and slug.
help.worktree-parent-dir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
help.runtime-services-json: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.max-turns-per-run: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
help.local-command: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extra-args: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.env-vars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrap-prompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
help.payload-template-json: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhook-url: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
help.heartbeat-interval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeout-sec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
help.grace-sec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
help.wake-on-demand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
help.cooldown-sec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.max-concurrent-runs: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
help.budget-monthly-cents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.
